3	class StatusPolicy < ApplicationPolicy
4	  def show?
5	    return false if author.unavailable?
6	
7	    if requires_mention?
8	      owned? || mention_exists?
9	    elsif private?
10	      owned? || following_author? || mention_exists?
11	    else
12	      current_account.nil? || (!author_blocking? && !author_blocking_domain?)
13	    end
14	  end
15	
16	  def quote?
17	    show? && !blocking_author? && record.quote_policy_for_account(current_account) != :denied
18	  end
19	
20	  def reblog?
21	    !requires_mention? && (!private? || owned?) && show? && !blocking_author?
22	  end
23	
24	  def favourite?
25	    show? && !blocking_author?
26	  end
27	
28	  def destroy?
29	    owned?
30	  end
31	
32	  alias unreblog? destroy?
33	
34	  def update?
35	    owned?
36	  end
37	
38	  private
39	
40	  def requires_mention?
41	    record.direct_visibility? || record.limited_visibility?
42	  end
43	
44	  def owned?
45	    author.id == current_account&.id
46	  end
47	
48	  def private?
49	    record.private_visibility?

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusPolicy < ApplicationPolicy
  def show?
    return false if author.unavailable?

    if requires_mention?
      owned? || mention_exists?
    elsif private?
      owned? || following_author? || mention_exists?
    else
      current_account.nil? || (!author_blocking? && !author_blocking_domain?)
    end
  end

  def quote?
    show? && !blocking_author? && record.quote_policy_for_account(current_account) != :denied
  end

  def reblog?
    !requires_mention? && (!private? || owned?) && show? && !blocking_author?
  end

  def favourite?
    show? && !blocking_author?
  end

  def destroy?
    owned?
  end

  alias unreblog? destroy?

  def update?
    owned?
  end

  private

  def requires_mention?
    record.direct_visibility? || record.limited_visibility?
  end

  def owned?
    author.id == current_account&.id
  end

  def private?
    record.private_visibility?
  end

  def mention_exists?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    if record.mentions.loaded?
      record.mentions.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == current_account.id }
    else
      record.mentions.exists?(account: current_account)
    end
  end

  def author_blocking_domain?
    return false if current_account.nil? || current_account.domain.nil?

    author.domain_blocking?(current_account.domain)
  end

  def blocking_author?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.blocking?(author)
  end

  def author_blocking?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.blocked_by?(author)
  end

  def following_author?
    return false if current_account.nil?

    current_account.following?(author)
  end

  def author
    record.account
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusReachFinder
  # @param [Status] status
  # @param [Hash] options
  # @option options [Boolean] :unsafe
  def initialize(status, options = {})
    @status  = status
    @options = options
  end

  def inboxes
    (reached_account_inboxes + followers_inboxes + relay_inboxes).uniq
  end

  private

  def reached_account_inboxes
    scope = Account.where(id: reached_account_ids)
    inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
  end

  def reached_account_ids
    # When the status is a reblog, there are no interactions with it
    # directly, we assume all interactions are with the original one

    if @status.reblog?
      [reblog_of_account_id]
    else
      [
        replied_to_account_id,
        reblog_of_account_id,
        quote_of_account_id,
        mentioned_account_ids,
        reblogs_account_ids,
        quotes_account_ids,
        favourites_account_ids,
        replies_account_ids,
      ].tap do |arr|
        arr.flatten!
        arr.compact!
        arr.uniq!
      end
    end
  end

  def replied_to_account_id
    @status.in_reply_to_account_id if distributable?
  end

  def quote_of_account_id
    @status.quote&.quoted_account_id
  end

  def reblog_of_account_id
    @status.reblog.account_id if @status.reblog?
  end

  def mentioned_account_ids
    @status.mentions.pluck(:account_id)
  end

  # Beware: Quotes can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def quotes_account_ids
    @status.quotes.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Reblogs can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def reblogs_account_ids
    @status.reblogs.rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at]).pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Favourites can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def favourites_account_ids
    @status.favourites.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  # Beware: Replies can be created without the author having had access to the status
  def replies_account_ids
    @status.replies.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
  end

  def followers_inboxes
    scope = followers_scope
    inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
  end

  def relay_inboxes
    if @status.public_visibility?
      Relay.enabled.pluck(:inbox_url)
    else
      []
    end
  end

  def distributable?
    @status.public_visibility? || @status.unlisted_visibility?
  end

  def unsafe?
    @options[:unsafe]
  end

  def followers_scope
    if @status.in_reply_to_local_account? && distributable?
      @status.account.followers.or(@status.thread.account.followers.not_domain_blocked_by_account(@status.account))
    elsif @status.direct_visibility? || @status.limited_visibility?
      Account.none
    else
      @status.account.followers
    end
  end

  def inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
    scope.merge!(Account.without_suspended) unless unsafe?
    scope.inboxes
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class BatchedRemoveStatusService < BaseService
  include Redisable

  # Delete multiple statuses and reblogs of them as efficiently as possible
  # @param [Enumerable<Status>] statuses An array of statuses
  # @param [Hash] options
  # @option [Boolean] :skip_side_effects Do not modify feeds and send updates to streaming API
  def call(statuses, **options)
    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
      records: statuses,
      associations: options[:skip_side_effects] ? :reblogs : [:account, :tags, reblogs: :account]
    ).call

    statuses_and_reblogs = statuses.flat_map { |status| [status] + status.reblogs }

    # The conversations for direct visibility statuses also need
    # to be manually updated. This part is not efficient but we
    # rely on direct visibility statuses being relatively rare.
    statuses_with_account_conversations = statuses.select(&:direct_visibility?)

    ActiveRecord::Associations::Preloader.new(
      records: statuses_with_account_conversations,
      associations: [mentions: :account]
    ).call

    statuses_with_account_conversations.each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)

    # We do not batch all deletes into one to avoid having a long-running
    # transaction lock the database, but we use the delete method instead
    # of destroy to avoid all callbacks. We rely on foreign keys to
    # cascade the delete faster without loading the associations.
    statuses_and_reblogs.each_slice(50) { |slice| Status.unscoped.where(id: slice.pluck(:id)).delete_all }

    # Since we skipped all callbacks, we also need to manually
    # deindex the statuses
    if Chewy.enabled?
      Chewy.strategy.current.update(StatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
      Chewy.strategy.current.update(PublicStatusesIndex, statuses_and_reblogs)
    end

    return if options[:skip_side_effects]

    # Batch by source account
    statuses_and_reblogs.group_by(&:account_id).each_value do |account_statuses|
      account = account_statuses.first.account

      next unless account

      unpush_from_home_timelines(account, account_statuses)
      unpush_from_list_timelines(account, account_statuses)
    end

    # Cannot be batched
    @status_id_cutoff = Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(2.weeks.ago)
    redis.pipelined do |pipeline|
      statuses.each do |status|
        unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
      end
    end
  end

  private

  def unpush_from_home_timelines(account, statuses)
    account.followers_for_local_distribution.includes(:user).reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
      statuses.each do |status|
        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_home(follower, status)
      end
    end
  end

  def unpush_from_list_timelines(account, statuses)
    account.lists_for_local_distribution.select(:id, :account_id).includes(account: :user).reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
      statuses.each do |status|
        FeedManager.instance.unpush_from_list(list, status)
      end
    end
  end

  def unpush_from_public_timelines(status, pipeline)
    return unless status.public_visibility? && status.id > @status_id_cutoff

    payload = { event: :delete, payload: status.id.to_s }.to_json

    pipeline.publish('timeline:public', payload)
    pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local' : 'timeline:public:remote', payload)

    if status.media_attachments.any?
      pipeline.publish('timeline:public:media', payload)
      pipeline.publish(status.local? ? 'timeline:public:local:media' : 'timeline:public:remote:media', payload)
    end

    status.tags.map { |tag| tag.name.downcase }.each do |hashtag|
      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}", payload)
      pipeline.publish("timeline:hashtag:#{hashtag}:local", payload) if status.local?
    end
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class Vacuum::StatusesVacuum
  include Redisable

  def initialize(retention_period)
    @retention_period = retention_period
  end

  def perform
    vacuum_statuses! if @retention_period.present?
  end

  private

  def vacuum_statuses!
    statuses_scope.in_batches do |statuses|
      # Side-effects not covered by foreign keys, such
      # as the search index, must be handled first.
      statuses.direct_visibility
        .includes(mentions: :account)
        .find_each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
      if Chewy.enabled?
        remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:StatusesIndex')
        remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:PublicStatusesIndex')
      end

      # Foreign keys take care of most associated records for us.
      # Media attachments will be orphaned.
      statuses.delete_all
    end
  end

  def statuses_scope
    Status.unscoped.kept
      .joins(:account).merge(Account.remote)
      .where(statuses: { id: ...retention_period_as_id })
  end

  def retention_period_as_id
    Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(@retention_period.ago, with_random: false)
  end

  def remove_from_index(status_ids, index)
    with_redis { |redis| redis.sadd(index, status_ids) }
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

class StatusFinder
  attr_reader :url

  def initialize(url)
    @url = url
  end

  def status
    verify_action!

    raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound unless TagManager.instance.local_url?(url)

    case recognized_params[:controller]
    when 'statuses'
      Status.find(recognized_params[:id])
    else
      raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
    end
  end

  private

  def recognized_params
    Rails.application.routes.recognize_path(url)
  end

  def verify_action!
    raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound unless recognized_params[:action] == 'show'
  end
end

3	module Status::FaspConcern
4	  extend ActiveSupport::Concern
5	
6	  included do
7	    after_commit :announce_new_content_to_subscribed_fasp, on: :create
8	    after_commit :announce_updated_content_to_subscribed_fasp, on: :update
9	    after_commit :announce_deleted_content_to_subscribed_fasp, on: :destroy
10	    after_commit :announce_trends_to_subscribed_fasp, on: :create
11	  end
12	
13	  private
14	
15	  def announce_new_content_to_subscribed_fasp
16	    return unless Mastodon::Feature.fasp_enabled?
17	    return unless account_indexable? && public_visibility?
18	
19	    # We need the uri here, but it is set in another `after_commit`
20	    # callback. Hooks included from modules are run before the ones
21	    # in the class itself and can neither be reordered nor is there
22	    # a way to declare dependencies.
23	    store_uri if uri.nil?
24	    Fasp::AnnounceContentLifecycleEventWorker.perform_async(uri, 'new')
25	  end
26	
27	  def announce_updated_content_to_subscribed_fasp
28	    return unless Mastodon::Feature.fasp_enabled?
29	    return unless account_indexable? && public_visibility?
30	
31	    Fasp::AnnounceContentLifecycleEventWorker.perform_async(uri, 'update')
32	  end
33	
34	  def announce_deleted_content_to_subscribed_fasp
35	    return unless Mastodon::Feature.fasp_enabled?
36	    return unless account_indexable? && public_visibility?
37	
38	    Fasp::AnnounceContentLifecycleEventWorker.perform_async(uri, 'delete')
39	  end
40	
41	  def announce_trends_to_subscribed_fasp
42	    return unless Mastodon::Feature.fasp_enabled?
43	    return unless account_indexable?
44	
45	    candidate_id, trend_source =

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Status::FaspConcern
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  included do
    after_commit :announce_new_content_to_subscribed_fasp, on: :create
    after_commit :announce_updated_content_to_subscribed_fasp, on: :update
    after_commit :announce_deleted_content_to_subscribed_fasp, on: :destroy
    after_commit :announce_trends_to_subscribed_fasp, on: :create
  end

  private

  def announce_new_content_to_subscribed_fasp
    return unless Mastodon::Feature.fasp_enabled?
    return unless account_indexable? && public_visibility?

    # We need the uri here, but it is set in another `after_commit`
    # callback. Hooks included from modules are run before the ones
    # in the class itself and can neither be reordered nor is there
    # a way to declare dependencies.
    store_uri if uri.nil?
    Fasp::AnnounceContentLifecycleEventWorker.perform_async(uri, 'new')
  end

  def announce_updated_content_to_subscribed_fasp
    return unless Mastodon::Feature.fasp_enabled?
    return unless account_indexable? && public_visibility?

    Fasp::AnnounceContentLifecycleEventWorker.perform_async(uri, 'update')
  end

  def announce_deleted_content_to_subscribed_fasp
    return unless Mastodon::Feature.fasp_enabled?
    return unless account_indexable? && public_visibility?

    Fasp::AnnounceContentLifecycleEventWorker.perform_async(uri, 'delete')
  end

  def announce_trends_to_subscribed_fasp
    return unless Mastodon::Feature.fasp_enabled?
    return unless account_indexable?

    candidate_id, trend_source =
      if reblog_of_id
        [reblog_of_id, 'reblog']
      elsif in_reply_to_id
        [in_reply_to_id, 'reply']
      end
    Fasp::AnnounceTrendWorker.perform_async(candidate_id, trend_source) if candidate_id
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Status::FetchRepliesConcern
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  # debounce fetching all replies to minimize DoS
  # Period to wait between fetching replies
  FETCH_REPLIES_COOLDOWN_MINUTES = 15.minutes
  # Period to wait after a post is first created before fetching its replies
  FETCH_REPLIES_INITIAL_WAIT_MINUTES = 5.minutes

  included do
    scope :created_recently, -> { where(created_at: FETCH_REPLIES_INITIAL_WAIT_MINUTES.ago..) }
    scope :not_created_recently, -> { where(created_at: ..FETCH_REPLIES_INITIAL_WAIT_MINUTES.ago) }
    scope :fetched_recently, -> { where(fetched_replies_at: FETCH_REPLIES_COOLDOWN_MINUTES.ago..) }
    scope :not_fetched_recently, -> { where(fetched_replies_at: [nil, ..FETCH_REPLIES_COOLDOWN_MINUTES.ago]) }

    scope :should_not_fetch_replies, -> { local.or(created_recently.or(fetched_recently)) }
    scope :should_fetch_replies, -> { remote.not_created_recently.not_fetched_recently }

    # statuses for which we won't receive update or deletion actions,
    # and should update when fetching replies
    # Status from an account which either
    # a) has only remote followers
    # b) has local follows that were created after the last update time, or
    # c) has no known followers
    scope :unsubscribed, lambda {
      remote.merge(
        Status.left_outer_joins(account: :followers).where.not(followers_accounts: { domain: nil })
              .or(where.not('follows.created_at < statuses.updated_at'))
              .or(where(follows: { id: nil }))
      )
    }
  end

  def should_fetch_replies?
    # we aren't brand new, and we haven't fetched replies since the debounce window
    !local? && distributable? && created_at <= FETCH_REPLIES_INITIAL_WAIT_MINUTES.ago && (
      fetched_replies_at.nil? || fetched_replies_at <= FETCH_REPLIES_COOLDOWN_MINUTES.ago
    )
  end
end

# frozen_string_literal: true

module Status::SafeReblogInsert
  extend ActiveSupport::Concern

  class_methods do
    # This patch overwrites the built-in ActiveRecord `_insert_record` method to
    # ensure that no reblogs of discarded statuses are created, as this cannot be
    # enforced through DB constraints the same way as reblogs of deleted statuses
    #
    # We redefine the internal method responsible for issuing the `INSERT`
    # statement and replace the `INSERT INTO ... VALUES ...` query with an `INSERT
    # INTO ... SELECT ...` query with a `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` clause on the
    # reblogged status to ensure consistency at the database level.
    #
    # The code is kept similar to ActiveRecord::Persistence code and calls it
    # directly when we are not handling a reblog.
    #
    # https://github.com/rails/rails/blob/v8.0.2/activerecord/lib/active_record/persistence.rb#L238-L261
    def _insert_record(connection, values, returning)
      return super unless values.is_a?(Hash) && values['reblog_of_id']&.value.present?

      primary_key = self.primary_key
      primary_key_value = nil

      if prefetch_primary_key? && primary_key
        values[primary_key] ||= begin
          primary_key_value = next_sequence_value
          _default_attributes[primary_key].with_cast_value(primary_key_value)
        end
      end

      # The following line departs from stock ActiveRecord
      # Original code was:
      # im = Arel::InsertManager.new(arel_table)
      # Instead, we use a custom builder when a reblog is happening:
      im = _compile_reblog_insert(values)

      connection.insert(
        im, "#{self} Create", primary_key || false, primary_key_value,
        returning: returning
      ).tap do |result|
        # Since we are using SELECT instead of VALUES, a non-error `nil` return is possible.
        # For our purposes, it's equivalent to a foreign key constraint violation
        raise ActiveRecord::InvalidForeignKey, "(reblog_of_id)=(#{values['reblog_of_id'].value}) is not present in table \"statuses\"" if result.nil?
      end
    end

    def _compile_reblog_insert(values)
      # This is somewhat equivalent to the following code of ActiveRecord::Persistence:
      # `arel_table.compile_insert(_substitute_values(values))`
      # The main difference is that we use a `SELECT` instead of a `VALUES` clause,
      # which means we have to build the `SELECT` clause ourselves and do a bit more
      # manual work.

      # Instead of using Arel::InsertManager#values, we are going to use Arel::InsertManager#select
      im = Arel::InsertManager.new
      im.into(arel_table)

      binds = []
      reblog_bind = nil
      values.each do |name, attribute|
        attr = arel_table[name]
        bind = predicate_builder.build_bind_attribute(attr.name, attribute.value)

        im.columns << attr
        binds << bind

        reblog_bind = bind if name == 'reblog_of_id'
      end

      im.select(arel_table.where(arel_table[:id].eq(reblog_bind)).where(arel_table[:deleted_at].eq(nil)).project(*binds))

      im
    end
  end
end

content.text:
I now have a complete, grounded picture across all seven steps. Here is the audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Mastodon, a federated (ActivityPub) social server. **Rails** monolith (Ruby, 3,205 files) with a **React/TypeScript** SPA front-end (`app/javascript`). Backend layered conventionally:

- `app/models` — ActiveRecord domain (incl. `app/models/concerns/` for mixins)
- `app/services` — write-side orchestration (`*Service`, called as `Service.new.call(...)`)
- `app/workers` — Sidekiq async jobs (`*Worker#perform`)
- `app/lib` — domain helpers (`ActivityPub::*`, feed managers, finders)
- `app/controllers` (REST + ActivityPub + admin), `app/serializers`, `app/policies` (Pundit), `app/presenters`

**Central content model:** `Status` — `app/models/status.rb:34` (`class Status < ApplicationRecord`). It is a top hub (124+ callers). Everything users post is a Status; feeds, notifications, federation, moderation and trends all revolve around it. The shared pieces it is built on are 10 `Status::*` concerns in `app/models/concerns/status/` plus 4 cross-cutting concerns (`Cacheable`, `Paginable`, `RateLimitable`, `Discard::Model`). Its closest collaborators: `Account` (author), `MediaAttachment`, `Poll`, `Conversation`, `Mention`, `StatusStat`, `Favourite`, `Bookmark`, `StatusPin`, `Quote`, `StatusEdit`, `Tag`.

---

## Step 2 — The Status contract and what it is built on

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb:34`. **Table** `statuses` (schema header lines 3–32): key columns `deleted_at` (soft-delete), `edited_at`, `uri`/`url`, `visibility` (enum), `reblog_of_id`, `in_reply_to_id`, `conversation_id`, `poll_id`, `account_id`, `quote_approval_policy`, `ordered_media_attachment_ids`.

**Concerns included** (`status.rb:35-46`):
- `Cacheable` — `app/models/concerns/cacheable.rb:3` — `cache_associated`/`with_includes`; sets `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS` preloading (status.rb:48-60, 180-184).
- `Discard::Model` (gem) — soft-delete; `discard_column = :deleted_at` (status.rb:66). **This is the teardown mechanism.**
- `Paginable` — `app/models/concerns/paginable.rb:3` — id-based pagination scopes.
- `RateLimitable` — `app/models/concerns/rate_limitable.rb:3` — `rate_limit by: :account, family: :statuses` (status.rb:64).
- `Status::FaspConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/fasp_concern.rb:3` — `after_commit` create/update/destroy hooks announcing content lifecycle + trends to FASP providers.
- `Status::FetchRepliesConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/fetch_replies_concern.rb:3` — `should_fetch_replies?` + `should_fetch_replies`/`unsubscribed` scopes (remote reply backfill).
- `Status::SafeReblogInsert` — `app/models/concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb:3` — overrides AR `_insert_record` so a reblog INSERT is gated by `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` on the reblogged status.
- `Status::SearchConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/search_concern.rb:3` — `indexable` scope, `searchable_by`/`searchable_text`/`searchable_properties` for Chewy/ES.
- `Status::SnapshotConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/snapshot_concern.rb:3` — `has_many :edits` (StatusEdit, `dependent: :destroy`), `build_snapshot`/`snapshot!`, `edited?`.
- `Status::ThreadingConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/threading_concern.rb:3` — recursive `ancestors`/`descendants`, `permitted_statuses_from_ids`, `self_replies`.
- `Status::Visibility` — `app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:3` — the `visibility` enum (public/unlisted/private/direct/limited), `distributable?`/`hidden?`/`sign?`, `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` scopes, `set_visibility`.
- `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/interaction_policy_concern.rb:3` — `quote_interaction_policy` (`composed_of`), `quote_policy_for_account`.

**Associations other records hang off it** (status.rb:75-118): `account`, `in_reply_to_account`, `conversation`, `preloadable_poll`/`poll`, self-joins `thread`/`reblog`/`replies`/`reblogs`; `favourites`, `bookmarks`, `mentions`/`active_mentions`, `media_attachments` (`dependent: :nullify`), `tagged_objects`, `quotes`/`quote`, `tags` (HABTM), `preview_cards_status`, `notification` (polymorphic `as: :activity`), `status_stat`, `trend` (StatusTrend), `edits`. Most use `dependent: :destroy`; **media is `:nullify`** (orphaned on delete — see vacuum), `status_stat`/`replies`/`trend` are `dependent: nil`.

**What a teammate must know before changing it:**
- `default_scope { recent.kept }` (status.rb:128) — every query silently excludes discarded rows; `kept` = `deleted_at IS NULL`. Teardown = `discard` (sets `deleted_at`), not destroy.
- `discard_with_reblogs` (status.rb:400) soft-deletes the status and all its reblogs in one stroke; `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!, prepend: true` (status.rb:178) must run before dependent-destroy.
- Counter caches (`increment/decrement_counter_caches`, status.rb:479-493) and `StatusStat` keep reblog/favourite/reply counts; visibility gates them (`direct` skipped).
- `proper` (status.rb:234) resolves reblog→original — pervasive assumption downstream.
- Snowflake IDs (`around_create`, status.rb:171) encode creation time; cleanup/vacuum rely on `id_at(time)`.
- Webhooks fire on commit (status.rb:157-158, 495-501); FASP hooks on commit (fasp_concern).

---

## Step 3 — Creation and distribution

| Layer | Location | Sync/Async |
|---|---|---|
| Posting entrypoint | `PostStatusService#call` `app/services/post_status_service.rb:42` | inline |
| Build + mentions/links/quote/antispam | `#process_status!` `post_status_service.rb:84-100` (ProcessMentionsService:86, ProcessLinksService via `attach_tagged_objects!`:121, `attach_quote!`:112) | inline |
| Persistence | `@status.save!` in a transaction `post_status_service.rb:97-99`; model callbacks fire (set_conversation, snowflake, store_uri, counter caches, webhooks) | inline |
| Post-processing fan-out trigger | `#postprocess_status!` `post_status_service.rb:162-171` | enqueues async |
| Hashtags / trends | `ProcessHashtagsService` + `Trends.tags.register` `post_status_service.rb:163-164` | inline |
| Link preview | `LinkCrawlWorker.perform_async` `post_status_service.rb:165` | **async** |
| **Local distribution (feeds/notifs)** | `DistributionWorker.perform_async` `:166` → `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:8` → `FanOutOnWriteService#call` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` | **async** |
| **Federation out** | `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker.perform_async` `:168` → `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:11` → `StatusReachFinder` inboxes + `ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker` | **async** |
| Poll expiry / quote request / email | `PollExpirationNotifyWorker`, `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker` `:169-170`, `EmailDistributionWorker` (`process_email_subscriptions!`:173) | **async** |

`FanOutOnWriteService` (sync, inside the worker): `deliver_to_self!`→`FeedManager#push_to_home` (fan_out:71); `notify_mentioned_accounts!`/`notify_quoted_account!`/`notify_about_update!` → `LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk` (fan_out:75-112, async); `deliver_to_all_followers!`/`deliver_to_lists!`/`deliver_to_hashtag_followers!` → `FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk` (fan_out:114-144, async); `broadcast_to_public_streams!`/`broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!` → Redis publish (fan_out:146-163, sync); `warm_payload_cache!` via `InlineRenderer` (fan_out:169-181). Inbound federation creation: `ActivityPub::Activity::Create#create_status` `app/lib/activitypub/activity/create.rb:17`.

## Step 5 — How status identity & visibility are guarded

**Existence / current (not torn-down):**
- `default_scope { recent.kept }` `status.rb:128` — baseline: discarded (`deleted_at`) rows never returned unless `.unscoped`/`.with_discarded`.
- `Status.find` raising `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound`, rescued in workers: `DistributionWorker#perform` `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:12`, `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker#perform` `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:16`.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#check_race_condition!` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:29-39` — raises `Mastodon::RaceConditionError` if `visibility.nil?` (re-queues).
- `Status::SafeReblogInsert._insert_record` `app/models/concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb:20` — DB-level `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` so you can't reblog a discarded status.
- `RemoveStatusService` `with_redis_lock("distribute:#{id}")` `remove_status_service.rb:22` and `DistributionWorker` `:9` — same lock key serializes distribution vs teardown.
- `StatusReachFinder#reblogs_account_ids` `status_reach_finder.rb:70` uses `rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at])` to include just-discarded reblogs in the delete reach.

**Visibility / authorization:**
- `Status::Visibility#distributable?`/`hidden?` `app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:31,27` and scopes `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` `:12-14` — the public/unlisted gate used everywhere.
- `StatusPolicy#show?` `app/policies/status_policy.rb:4` — checks `author.unavailable?`, mention-required (direct/limited), private→following/owned/mentioned, blocks/domain-blocks. `quote?:16`, `reblog?:20`, `favourite?:24`, `destroy?/update?:28,34` (`owned?`).
- `StatusFilter#filtered?` `app/lib/status_filter.rb:11` — wraps `StatusPolicy#show?` plus block/mute/domain-block/silenced; used by `Status::ThreadingConcern#permitted_statuses_from_ids` `threading_concern.rb:14`.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#broadcastable?` `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:188` (`public_visibility? && !reblog? && !silenced?`) and `@status.proper.account.suspended?` early-return `:17`.
- `StatusReachFinder#distributable?` `status_reach_finder.rb:96` + `inboxes_without_suspended_for`/`Account.without_suspended` `:114-116` (unless `unsafe:`) — federation reach respects visibility and suspension; `relay_inboxes` only for public `:88`.
- Teardown visibility gates: `RemoveStatusService#remove_from_public/_media/_hashtags` guard on `public_visibility?` `remove_status_service.rb:124,135,144`; `permanently?` `:162` keeps reported statuses; `BatchedRemoveStatusService#unpush_from_public_timelines` guards `public_visibility?` `:83`.
- Account-side gating in counter caches/distribution: `increment/decrement_counter_caches` skip `direct_visibility?` `status.rb:480,488`; `update_statistics`/`from_text` require `distributable?` `:474,391`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

`sense_blast(Status)` = **risk: high**, 152 direct callers, **318 affected symbols across 104 files**, 198 production symbols. Areas at risk:

**Highest — break = data loss / silent visibility leak / federation breakage:**
1. **Teardown chain** — `RemoveStatusService` `remove_status_service.rb:16`, `BatchedRemoveStatusService` `batched_remove_status_service.rb:10`, `Status#discard_with_reblogs:400`, `#unlink_from_conversations!:406`/`before_destroy :prepend`:178. Changing how `deleted_at`/`discard` works directly rewrites these; the `default_scope … kept` (`status.rb:128`) coupling means any change to discard semantics silently changes *every* read.
2. **`Status::SafeReblogInsert`** `safe_reblog_insert.rb:20` — patches AR internals against a specific Rails version; touching insert/teardown can resurrect reblogs of deleted posts.
3. **`Status::Visibility`** `visibility.rb:3` + `StatusPolicy`/`StatusFilter` — any change to the visibility enum or `distributable?` re-flows through feeds, federation reach (`StatusReachFinder`), public broadcast, and authorization. A mistake leaks private/direct posts.
4. **Federation hard-delete reliance on FKs** — `Vacuum::StatusesVacuum` `statuses_vacuum.rb:30` and `BatchedRemoveStatusService` `:34` use `delete_all` and rely on DB foreign keys + `dependent` options to cascade. Changing associations/`dependent:` (esp. `media_attachments dependent: :nullify` `status.rb:96`) orphans media or leaves dangling rows.
5. **Async distribution** — `DistributionWorker`/`FanOutOnWriteService`/`ActivityPub::DistributionWorker`. The lock-key + `Status.find` + `check_race_condition!` invariants must survive; breaking them drops posts from feeds or duplicates deliveries.

**High — break = wrong counts / missing notifications / moderation gaps:**
6. Counter caches & `StatusStat` (`increment/decrement_count!` `status.rb:324-342`, callbacks `:160-161`).
7. Notifications: `Notification#target_status:150`, `NotifyService:76`, `LocalNotificationWorker`.
8. Moderation/trends: `StatusPolicy`, `StatusFilter`, `Report#statuses:78`, `Admin::StatusBatchAction`, `Trends::Statuses`.
9. Search: `Status::SearchConcern` + `update_index` (status.rb:72-73), `Importer::*StatusesIndexImporter`, Chewy deindex in vacuum/batched-remove.

**Medium — break = degraded UX / specific endpoints:**
10. Read paths: `StatusCacheHydrator`, `REST::StatusSerializer`, timeline/profile controllers, `StatusFinder`/`PermalinkRedirector`/`ResolveURLService`.
11. Maintenance/export: `AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy`, `Scheduler::UserCleanupScheduler`, `BackupService`, `Account::Merging#merge_with!`, CLI tasks.
12. FASP: `Status::FaspConcern`, `Fasp::BackfillRequest`.

**Must re-verify after change:** soft-delete vs hard-delete semantics under `default_scope`; reblog/quote/reply cascades; media orphaning; counter-cache accuracy; visibility never widened; federation delete reach (`StatusReachFinder` unsafe path); search (de)indexing; conversation unlinking for direct posts; the `SafeReblogInsert` Rails-internal patch against the pinned Rails version.

result:
I now have a complete, grounded picture across all seven steps. Here is the audit.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**Project:** Mastodon, a federated (ActivityPub) social server. **Rails** monolith (Ruby, 3,205 files) with a **React/TypeScript** SPA front-end (`app/javascript`). Backend layered conventionally:

- `app/models` — ActiveRecord domain (incl. `app/models/concerns/` for mixins)
- `app/services` — write-side orchestration (`*Service`, called as `Service.new.call(...)`)
- `app/workers` — Sidekiq async jobs (`*Worker#perform`)
- `app/lib` — domain helpers (`ActivityPub::*`, feed managers, finders)
- `app/controllers` (REST + ActivityPub + admin), `app/serializers`, `app/policies` (Pundit), `app/presenters`

**Central content model:** `Status` — `app/models/status.rb:34` (`class Status < ApplicationRecord`). It is a top hub (124+ callers). Everything users post is a Status; feeds, notifications, federation, moderation and trends all revolve around it. The shared pieces it is built on are 10 `Status::*` concerns in `app/models/concerns/status/` plus 4 cross-cutting concerns (`Cacheable`, `Paginable`, `RateLimitable`, `Discard::Model`). Its closest collaborators: `Account` (author), `MediaAttachment`, `Poll`, `Conversation`, `Mention`, `StatusStat`, `Favourite`, `Bookmark`, `StatusPin`, `Quote`, `StatusEdit`, `Tag`.

---

## Step 2 — The Status contract and what it is built on

**Model:** `app/models/status.rb:34`. **Table** `statuses` (schema header lines 3–32): key columns `deleted_at` (soft-delete), `edited_at`, `uri`/`url`, `visibility` (enum), `reblog_of_id`, `in_reply_to_id`, `conversation_id`, `poll_id`, `account_id`, `quote_approval_policy`, `ordered_media_attachment_ids`.

**Concerns included** (`status.rb:35-46`):
- `Cacheable` — `app/models/concerns/cacheable.rb:3` — `cache_associated`/`with_includes`; sets `CACHEABLE_ASSOCIATIONS` preloading (status.rb:48-60, 180-184).
- `Discard::Model` (gem) — soft-delete; `discard_column = :deleted_at` (status.rb:66). **This is the teardown mechanism.**
- `Paginable` — `app/models/concerns/paginable.rb:3` — id-based pagination scopes.
- `RateLimitable` — `app/models/concerns/rate_limitable.rb:3` — `rate_limit by: :account, family: :statuses` (status.rb:64).
- `Status::FaspConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/fasp_concern.rb:3` — `after_commit` create/update/destroy hooks announcing content lifecycle + trends to FASP providers.
- `Status::FetchRepliesConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/fetch_replies_concern.rb:3` — `should_fetch_replies?` + `should_fetch_replies`/`unsubscribed` scopes (remote reply backfill).
- `Status::SafeReblogInsert` — `app/models/concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb:3` — overrides AR `_insert_record` so a reblog INSERT is gated by `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` on the reblogged status.
- `Status::SearchConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/search_concern.rb:3` — `indexable` scope, `searchable_by`/`searchable_text`/`searchable_properties` for Chewy/ES.
- `Status::SnapshotConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/snapshot_concern.rb:3` — `has_many :edits` (StatusEdit, `dependent: :destroy`), `build_snapshot`/`snapshot!`, `edited?`.
- `Status::ThreadingConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/threading_concern.rb:3` — recursive `ancestors`/`descendants`, `permitted_statuses_from_ids`, `self_replies`.
- `Status::Visibility` — `app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:3` — the `visibility` enum (public/unlisted/private/direct/limited), `distributable?`/`hidden?`/`sign?`, `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` scopes, `set_visibility`.
- `Status::InteractionPolicyConcern` — `app/models/concerns/status/interaction_policy_concern.rb:3` — `quote_interaction_policy` (`composed_of`), `quote_policy_for_account`.

**Associations other records hang off it** (status.rb:75-118): `account`, `in_reply_to_account`, `conversation`, `preloadable_poll`/`poll`, self-joins `thread`/`reblog`/`replies`/`reblogs`; `favourites`, `bookmarks`, `mentions`/`active_mentions`, `media_attachments` (`dependent: :nullify`), `tagged_objects`, `quotes`/`quote`, `tags` (HABTM), `preview_cards_status`, `notification` (polymorphic `as: :activity`), `status_stat`, `trend` (StatusTrend), `edits`. Most use `dependent: :destroy`; **media is `:nullify`** (orphaned on delete — see vacuum), `status_stat`/`replies`/`trend` are `dependent: nil`.

**What a teammate must know before changing it:**
- `default_scope { recent.kept }` (status.rb:128) — every query silently excludes discarded rows; `kept` = `deleted_at IS NULL`. Teardown = `discard` (sets `deleted_at`), not destroy.
- `discard_with_reblogs` (status.rb:400) soft-deletes the status and all its reblogs in one stroke; `before_destroy :unlink_from_conversations!, prepend: true` (status.rb:178) must run before dependent-destroy.
- Counter caches (`increment/decrement_counter_caches`, status.rb:479-493) and `StatusStat` keep reblog/favourite/reply counts; visibility gates them (`direct` skipped).
- `proper` (status.rb:234) resolves reblog→original — pervasive assumption downstream.
- Snowflake IDs (`around_create`, status.rb:171) encode creation time; cleanup/vacuum rely on `id_at(time)`.
- Webhooks fire on commit (status.rb:157-158, 495-501); FASP hooks on commit (fasp_concern).

---

## Step 3 — Creation and distribution

| Layer | Location | Sync/Async |
|---|---|---|
| Posting entrypoint | `PostStatusService#call` `app/services/post_status_service.rb:42` | inline |
| Build + mentions/links/quote/antispam | `#process_status!` `post_status_service.rb:84-100` (ProcessMentionsService:86, ProcessLinksService via `attach_tagged_objects!`:121, `attach_quote!`:112) | inline |
| Persistence | `@status.save!` in a transaction `post_status_service.rb:97-99`; model callbacks fire (set_conversation, snowflake, store_uri, counter caches, webhooks) | inline |
| Post-processing fan-out trigger | `#postprocess_status!` `post_status_service.rb:162-171` | enqueues async |
| Hashtags / trends | `ProcessHashtagsService` + `Trends.tags.register` `post_status_service.rb:163-164` | inline |
| Link preview | `LinkCrawlWorker.perform_async` `post_status_service.rb:165` | **async** |
| **Local distribution (feeds/notifs)** | `DistributionWorker.perform_async` `:166` → `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:8` → `FanOutOnWriteService#call` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` | **async** |
| **Federation out** | `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker.perform_async` `:168` → `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:11` → `StatusReachFinder` inboxes + `ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker` | **async** |
| Poll expiry / quote request / email | `PollExpirationNotifyWorker`, `ActivityPub::QuoteRequestWorker` `:169-170`, `EmailDistributionWorker` (`process_email_subscriptions!`:173) | **async** |

`FanOutOnWriteService` (sync, inside the worker): `deliver_to_self!`→`FeedManager#push_to_home` (fan_out:71); `notify_mentioned_accounts!`/`notify_quoted_account!`/`notify_about_update!` → `LocalNotificationWorker.push_bulk` (fan_out:75-112, async); `deliver_to_all_followers!`/`deliver_to_lists!`/`deliver_to_hashtag_followers!` → `FeedInsertWorker.push_bulk` (fan_out:114-144, async); `broadcast_to_public_streams!`/`broadcast_to_hashtag_streams!` → Redis publish (fan_out:146-163, sync); `warm_payload_cache!` via `InlineRenderer` (fan_out:169-181). Inbound federation creation: `ActivityPub::Activity::Create#create_status` `app/lib/activitypub/activity/create.rb:17`.

---

## Step 4 — Every dependent of the Status contract (grouped)

### Feeds & timelines
- `FanOutOnWriteService` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:12` — fans a new/edited status into home/list/public/hashtag feeds.
- `FeedManager#clear_from_home` `app/lib/feed_manager.rb:231`, `#clear_from_list` `:251`, `#clear_from_lists` `:271` — purge a status from feeds.
- `FeedInsertWorker#perform` `app/workers/feed_insert_worker.rb:7` — inserts one status into one feed.
- `PushUpdateWorker#perform` `app/workers/push_update_worker.rb:7` — pushes status payload to streaming.
- `Feed#from_redis` `app/models/feed.rb:22` / `#get` `:11` — rehydrates statuses from feed id lists.
- `PublicFeed` scopes `app/models/public_feed.rb:88,92,96,100,104,108,112,116` — public/local/remote/replies/reblogs/media/language/account filters.
- `TagFeed#tagged_with_any/all/none_scope` `app/models/tag_feed.rb:51,55,59` — hashtag timeline.
- `LinkFeed#attached_to_preview_card` `app/models/link_feed.rb:42` — link timeline.
- `AccountStatusesFilter` `app/lib/account_statuses_filter.rb:34,48,63,78,82,86,90,94` — per-account profile timeline scoping.
- `StatusCacheHydrator#fill_status_payload` `app/lib/status_cache_hydrator.rb:55` (+ `hydrate_reblog_payload:35`, `hydrate_non_reblog_payload:29`) — builds cached REST payload.
- `StatusRelationshipsPresenter#initialize` `app/presenters/status_relationships_presenter.rb:9` — maps favourited/reblogged/bookmarked/pinned/muted.
- Timeline controllers: `Api::V1::Timelines::HomeController#preloaded_home_statuses` `:31`, `ListController:27`, `TagController:25`, `PublicController:31`, `LinkController:24`.
- `AccountsController#show:17`, `#only_media_scope:54`, `#no_replies_scope:58`, `#hashtag_scope:62` (profile HTML).
- `Api::V1::Timelines::*`, `TagsController#set_statuses` `app/controllers/tags_controller.rb:47`.

### Federation (ActivityPub)

## Step 5 — How status identity & visibility are guarded

**Existence / current (not torn-down):**
- `default_scope { recent.kept }` `status.rb:128` — baseline: discarded (`deleted_at`) rows never returned unless `.unscoped`/`.with_discarded`.
- `Status.find` raising `ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound`, rescued in workers: `DistributionWorker#perform` `app/workers/distribution_worker.rb:12`, `ActivityPub::DistributionWorker#perform` `app/workers/activitypub/distribution_worker.rb:16`.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#check_race_condition!` `app/services/fan_out_on_write_service.rb:29-39` — raises `Mastodon::RaceConditionError` if `visibility.nil?` (re-queues).
- `Status::SafeReblogInsert._insert_record` `app/models/concerns/status/safe_reblog_insert.rb:20` — DB-level `WHERE deleted_at IS NULL` so you can't reblog a discarded status.
- `RemoveStatusService` `with_redis_lock("distribute:#{id}")` `remove_status_service.rb:22` and `DistributionWorker` `:9` — same lock key serializes distribution vs teardown.
- `StatusReachFinder#reblogs_account_ids` `status_reach_finder.rb:70` uses `rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at])` to include just-discarded reblogs in the delete reach.

**Visibility / authorization:**
- `Status::Visibility#distributable?`/`hidden?` `app/models/concerns/status/visibility.rb:31,27` and scopes `distributable_visibility`/`list_eligible_visibility`/`not_direct_visibility` `:12-14` — the public/unlisted gate used everywhere.
- `StatusPolicy#show?` `app/policies/status_policy.rb:4` — checks `author.unavailable?`, mention-required (direct/limited), private→following/owned/mentioned, blocks/domain-blocks. `quote?:16`, `reblog?:20`, `favourite?:24`, `destroy?/update?:28,34` (`owned?`).
- `StatusFilter#filtered?` `app/lib/status_filter.rb:11` — wraps `StatusPolicy#show?` plus block/mute/domain-block/silenced; used by `Status::ThreadingConcern#permitted_statuses_from_ids` `threading_concern.rb:14`.
- `FanOutOnWriteService#broadcastable?` `fan_out_on_write_service.rb:188` (`public_visibility? && !reblog? && !silenced?`) and `@status.proper.account.suspended?` early-return `:17`.
- `StatusReachFinder#distributable?` `status_reach_finder.rb:96` + `inboxes_without_suspended_for`/`Account.without_suspended` `:114-116` (unless `unsafe:`) — federation reach respects visibility and suspension; `relay_inboxes` only for public `:88`.
- Teardown visibility gates: `RemoveStatusService#remove_from_public/_media/_hashtags` guard on `public_visibility?` `remove_status_service.rb:124,135,144`; `permanently?` `:162` keeps reported statuses; `BatchedRemoveStatusService#unpush_from_public_timelines` guards `public_visibility?` `:83`.
- Account-side gating in counter caches/distribution: `increment/decrement_counter_caches` skip `direct_visibility?` `status.rb:480,488`; `update_statistics`/`from_text` require `distributable?` `:474,391`.

## Step 6 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

`sense_blast(Status)` = **risk: high**, 152 direct callers, **318 affected symbols across 104 files**, 198 production symbols. Areas at risk:

**Highest — break = data loss / silent visibility leak / federation breakage:**
1. **Teardown chain** — `RemoveStatusService` `remove_status_service.rb:16`, `BatchedRemoveStatusService` `batched_remove_status_service.rb:10`, `Status#discard_with_reblogs:400`, `#unlink_from_conversations!:406`/`before_destroy :prepend`:178. Changing how `deleted_at`/`discard` works directly rewrites these; the `default_scope … kept` (`status.rb:128`) coupling means any change to discard semantics silently changes *every* read.
2. **`Status::SafeReblogInsert`** `safe_reblog_insert.rb:20` — patches AR internals against a specific Rails version; touching insert/teardown can resurrect reblogs of deleted posts.
3. **`Status::Visibility`** `visibility.rb:3` + `StatusPolicy`/`StatusFilter` — any change to the visibility enum or `distributable?` re-flows through feeds, federation reach (`StatusReachFinder`), public broadcast, and authorization. A mistake leaks private/direct posts.
4. **Federation hard-delete reliance on FKs** — `Vacuum::StatusesVacuum` `statuses_vacuum.rb:30` and `BatchedRemoveStatusService` `:34` use `delete_all` and rely on DB foreign keys + `dependent` options to cascade. Changing associations/`dependent:` (esp. `media_attachments dependent: :nullify` `status.rb:96`) orphans media or leaves dangling rows.
5. **Async distribution** — `DistributionWorker`/`FanOutOnWriteService`/`ActivityPub::DistributionWorker`. The lock-key + `Status.find` + `check_race_condition!` invariants must survive; breaking them drops posts from feeds or duplicates deliveries.

**High — break = wrong counts / missing notifications / moderation gaps:**
6. Counter caches & `StatusStat` (`increment/decrement_count!` `status.rb:324-342`, callbacks `:160-161`).
7. Notifications: `Notification#target_status:150`, `NotifyService:76`, `LocalNotificationWorker`.
8. Moderation/trends: `StatusPolicy`, `StatusFilter`, `Report#statuses:78`, `Admin::StatusBatchAction`, `Trends::Statuses`.
9. Search: `Status::SearchConcern` + `update_index` (status.rb:72-73), `Importer::*StatusesIndexImporter`, Chewy deindex in vacuum/batched-remove.

**Medium — break = degraded UX / specific endpoints:**
10. Read paths: `StatusCacheHydrator`, `REST::StatusSerializer`, timeline/profile controllers, `StatusFinder`/`PermalinkRedirector`/`ResolveURLService`.
11. Maintenance/export: `AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy`, `Scheduler::UserCleanupScheduler`, `BackupService`, `Account::Merging#merge_with!`, CLI tasks.
12. FASP: `Status::FaspConcern`, `Fasp::BackfillRequest`.

**Must re-verify after change:** soft-delete vs hard-delete semantics under `default_scope`; reblog/quote/reply cascades; media orphaning; counter-cache accuracy; visibility never widened; federation delete reach (`StatusReachFinder` unsafe path); search (de)indexing; conversation unlinking for direct posts; the `SafeReblogInsert` Rails-internal patch against the pinned Rails version.
